input.background: An SRS of 18 male students at a university has an average height of 70 inches. The average height of men in the general population is 69 inches. Assume that male height is approximately Normally distributed with σ=2.8 inches.
input.question: Conduct a two-sided hypothesis test to determine whether the male students are significantly taller than expected. Show all hypothesis testing steps.

output.answer: The four step procedure is used to solve the problem: A. Hypotheses. $H_0: \mu=69$ versus $H_a: \mu \neq 69$ B. Test statistic. $z_{\text {stat }}=1.52$ C. $P$-value. One-sided $P=\operatorname{Pr}(Z \geq 1.52)=0.0643$. Therefore, the two-sided $P= 2 \times 0.0643=0.1286$. This is considered to be nonsignificant by usual conventions. D. Significance level (optional). Results are not significant at $\alpha=0.10$ (retain $H_0$ ).

sample_mean_height.description: The average height of the sample of 18 male students.

population_mean_height.description: The average height of men in the general population.

standard_deviation.description: The standard deviation of male height in the general population.

sample_size.description: The number of male students in the sample.
